input.prompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

data.content:
## Migrating MSTest 3.8.0 to Microsoft.Testing.Platform

### 1. Add a `Directory.Build.props` (consistent across all projects)

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <UseTestingPlatform>true</UseTestingPlatform>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Place this at or above the solution root so all three test projects inherit it.

- **`UseTestingPlatform`** – switches MSTest to the new platform runner (replaces VSTest).
- **`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`** – keeps `dotnet test` working (it bridges the new platform back to the `dotnet test` CLI).

### 2. Per-project changes

No `.csproj` edits are required if you use the `Directory.Build.props` above. Just ensure each project already references:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
```

(The unified `MSTest` package at ≥3.4 bundles the platform support.)

If any project still references the legacy split packages (`MSTest.TestAdapter` + `MSTest.TestFramework`), replace them with the single `MSTest` 3.8.0 package.

### 3. CI command change

```bash
# Before
dotnet test --logger trx

# After
dotnet test --logger trx
```

The command stays the same — that's what `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` enables. The TRX logger is built into Microsoft.Testing.Platform, so it continues to work.

### Summary of what each property does

| Property | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `UseTestingPlatform` | Opts into the new test host (each test project becomes its own executable) |
| `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` | Lets `dotnet test` orchestrate the new platform executables |

No test code changes are needed — MSTest attributes (`[TestClass]`, `[TestMethod]`, etc.) work identically on both runners.

Let me provide a concise answer covering the required changes.
